steps.title: Use tangent properties and perimeter to find the tangent segment length
steps.content:
Tangent lines to a circle are perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency, so $\angle GMH = \angle GNH = 90°$. Also, tangent segments from an external point to a circle are equal, so $HM = HN$.

The perimeter of quadrilateral $GMHN$ is:
$$GM + MH + HN + NG = 3,856$$
Since $GM = GN = 168$ (both radii) and $MH = HN = t$ (equal tangent segments), we have:
$$168 + t + t + 168 = 3,856$$
$$336 + 2t = 3,856$$
$$2t = 3,520$$
$$t = 1,760 \text{ mm}$$

steps.title: Apply Pythagorean theorem to find GH
steps.content:
In right triangle $GMH$ with the right angle at $M$, we use the Pythagorean theorem:
$$GH^2 = GM^2 + MH^2$$
$$GH^2 = 168^2 + 1,760^2$$
$$GH^2 = 28,224 + 3,097,600$$
$$GH^2 = 3,125,824$$
$$GH = 1,768 \text{ mm}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
